zh.Anlaby, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾安拉比
zh.Maddington, Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾阿姆斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Le Moyne of Maddington於大約1275年出生在英國維爾爾阿姆斯波利時，他的父親Hugh Le Moyne27歲，母親Alicia le Rus31歲。他於1299年與Alice Trenchard在英國維爾爾馬丁頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1340年在英國戴文默頓享年67歲。

zh-hans.Anlaby, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾安拉比
zh-hans.Maddington, Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英国維爾爾阿姆斯波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Le Moyne of Maddington于大约1275年出生在英国維爾爾阿姆斯波利时，他的父亲Hugh Le Moyne27岁，母亲Alicia le Rus31岁。他于1299年与Alice Trenchard在英国維爾爾馬丁頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1340年在英国戴文默頓享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Moyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’. Some of the following early bearers without the definite article could alternatively belong with (2).
2  English:  see  Moon   (1).

variants.name: Moin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Moyne, a nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): unexplained.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: Irish: variant of   Mohan  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Moynan
meanings: Irish: perhaps a diminutive of   Moyne   or a reduced form of   Moynihan  .

variants.name: Moine
meanings: French: from moine ‘monk’, probably applied as an occupational name for a servant at a monastery or an ironic nickname for someone of monkish behavior or appearance. Compare Lemoine .

variants.name: Moynes
meanings: English: variant of Moyne with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William Le Moyne of Maddington was born about 1275, in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, his father, Hugh Le Moyne, was 27 and his mother, Alicia le Rus, was 31. He married Alice Trenchard in 1299, in Maddington, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died about 1340, in Merton, Devon, England, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Le Moyne of Maddington est né environ 1275, à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Le Moyne, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alicia le Rus, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Alice Trenchard en 1299, à Maddington, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1340, à Merton, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Le Moyne of Maddington um 1275 in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh Le Moyne 27 und seine Mutter Alicia le Rus 31. Er hat 1299 in Maddington, Wiltshire, England Alice Trenchard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1340 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Merton, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Le Moyne of Maddington è nato il circa 1275 nel Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Hugh Le Moyne, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Alicia le Rus, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Alice Trenchard nel 1299, nel Maddington, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1340, nel Merton, Devon, England, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Le Moyne of Maddingtonが約1275年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーで生まれた時，父Hugh Le Moyneは27歳，母Alicia le Rusは31歳であった。1299年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアマッドイントンでAlice Trenchardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1340年にイギリスデボンマートンで67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Le Moyne of Maddington(이)가 대략 1275년 영국 윌츠히르 아메스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Le Moyne(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia le Rus(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1299년 영국 윌츠히르 매딩턴에서 Alice Trenchard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1340년 영국 데폰 머턴에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Le Moyne of Maddington родился около 1275 г. в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hugh Le Moyne было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia le Rus, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Trenchard в 1299 г. в местности мэддингтон, уилцхир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1340 г. в местности миртон, девон, Англия в возрасте 67 лет.
